patch.value:
## Context

`src/cli/commands/history-compact.ts` is well-designed for single-item compaction. `pm stats --storage` surfaces which streams are oversized. There is no bridge between the two: no `--all-over <N>`, `--all-before <timestamp>`, or batch mode.

Historically related: pm-3pbq (shipped pm history-compact) — closed. The bulk variant was not in scope.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pm history-compact --all-over <N>` compacts every stream with more than N entries, defaulting to a configurable threshold
- [ ] OR expose `pm history-compact --ids id1,id2,...` to support scripted bulk compaction
- [ ] Progress output shows per-item result (changed/noop/error)
- [ ] `--dry-run` supported for the bulk path
- [ ] Max concurrent operations bounded (sequential is fine for initial ship)

## Evidence

- `src/cli/commands/history-compact.ts:214` — `runHistoryCompact` takes a single `id` string
- `src/core/history/history-storage-stats.ts:81` — `computeHistoryStorageStats` already has `deepest_by_lines` for bulk selection
- `node dist/cli.js stats --storage` shows pm-th6y (686 entries), pm-wo8 (498 entries), pm-2c8 (155 entries) as prime candidates
History stream bulk-compaction: pm history-compact --all-over N to batch-compact large streams

pm history-compact operates on a single item ID. There is no bulk mode to compact all streams exceeding a threshold in one invocation. With 1057 items, a maintainer wanting to compact all streams older than a date or over N entries must script individual calls.

- `pm history-compact --all-over <N>` compacts every stream with more than N entries, defaulting to a configurable threshold
- OR expose `pm history-compact --ids id1,id2,...` to support scripted bulk compaction
- Progress output shows per-item result (changed/noop/error)
- `--dry-run` supported for the bulk path
- Max concurrent operations bounded (sequential is fine for initial ship)
